reason: The text contains multiple named regular human characters with professions and extensive dialogue. Key examples include: Pharaoh (פַּרְעֹה) who is the ruler of Egypt with substantial dialogue throughout; Moses (מֹשֶׁה) and Aaron (אַהֲרֹן) who are named individuals with the profession of prophet/leader, engaging in frequent dialogue with God and Pharaoh; Egyptian magicians (חַרְטֻמִּים) who are professionals with dialogue; Pharaoh's servants (עֲבָדָיו) who speak; and the elders of Israel (זִקְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל) who are called by Moses. These characters interact with one another throughout the narrative with named dialogue and identifiable roles.
